seoTitle: Add an Expense in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to record a business expense in QuickBooks Online, including creating a new expense category on the fly and attaching receipt files.
intro: Logging out-of-pocket purchases or debit card charges accurately keeps your tax deductions and cost tracking in order. QuickBooks Online's Expense form lets you record a transaction, create a missing category on the spot, and attach a digital receipt without leaving the screen. Here's the full workflow.

steps.heading: Open the Expense form
steps.body: Click + New, then select Expense under the Vendors column.

steps.heading: Choose your payee and account
steps.body: Select an existing vendor or type a new name to add it instantly, then choose the payment account used to fund the purchase.

steps.heading: Select a category
steps.body: In the Category Details section, open the category menu to choose the expense type.

steps.heading: Create a missing category if needed
steps.body: Click + Add New from the account list to open the Chart of Accounts wizard, set the type to Expenses, name it, and save.

steps.heading: Add amount, memo, and receipt
steps.body: Enter the exact payment amount, write a note in the memo box, drag your digital receipt into Attachments, then click Save and Close.

faq.question: Can I create a new expense category while entering a transaction?
faq.answer: Yes, clicking + Add New from the category dropdown opens the Chart of Accounts wizard so you can create it without leaving the form.

faq.question: How do I attach a receipt to an expense?
faq.answer: Drag and drop the receipt image or PDF into the Attachments panel tray at the bottom of the Expense form.

faq.question: Can I tag an expense to a specific project or customer?
faq.answer: Yes, advanced classification fields let you tag items to unique project rows or customer profiles when tracking is enabled.

faq.question: What payment methods can I record an expense against?
faq.answer: You can select checking, credit card, or cash accounts as the payment method used to fund the purchase.
